elements.sent-2411-003: Stationary Gaussian fields are a dominant model to represent spatially homogeneous continuous data in several dimensions.
elements.sent-2411-004: In particular, the {\it excursion sets , or {\it upper level sets , obtained by thresholding at a given level, are the topic of intensive research across several fields, and under many angles: image analysis, percolation, materials science, neurology, cosmology.}}
elements.sent-2411-005: Their geometric characteristics, such as the volume, perimeter, or topological indexes, have been the subject of several works on limit theorems.

elements.sent-2411-007: Recently, a large variety of disciplines have adopted methods from the mathematical domain of topological data analysis (TDA) in order to analyze data exhibiting complex topological features.
elements.sent-2411-008: In this area, the (persistent) Betti numbers are the key tool, which, loosely speaking, describe the number of holes of a fixed dimension contained in a considered data set.
elements.sent-2411-009: In order to put TDA on a rigorous statistical foundation and to allow for the derivation of hypothesis tests, it is crucial to develop the theory of normal approximation for the Betti numbers.
elements.sent-2411-010: This has been successfully achieved for topology models based on the Poisson point process \cite{shirai,b1 .}
elements.sent-2411-011: While point process models are interesting, when looking into applications, random-field models are of central importance \cite{astro2,astro1,torquato .}
elements.sent-2411-012: However, when considering the asymptotics of persistent Betti numbers, then much less is known.
elements.sent-2411-013: One exception is a work on discretely-indexed Gaussian excursions, which is however restricted to the sparse regime \cite{thoppe .}
elements.sent-2411-014: Central limit theorems (CLTs) for geometric functionals have been a topic of intensive research in the last 20 years.
elements.sent-2411-015: Here, the most thoroughly studied model is that of a Poisson point process in Euclidean space.

elements.sent-2411-017: Motivated by the emerging domain of TDA, we focus in this work on topological indexes of the fields.
elements.sent-2411-018: While there has been a lot of progress in the normal approximation of geometric functionals on Poisson processes, the situation is much less clear for the case of Gaussian fields.
elements.sent-2411-019: The first such work is the CLT for the Euler characteristic established by Estrade \& L\'eon \cite{leon .}
